steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage states swords were first developed in Britain during the Middle Bronze Age (approximately 14th–10th century BCE). Two hoards are given: Llanarmon-yn-Iâl from the 16th century BCE (before swords existed) and Horsehope Craig from the 7th century BCE (after swords existed). The blank must complete the contrast "evidence of sword production was found in ____" in a way explained by when swords were first developed.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'both the Llanarmon-yn-Iâl hoard and the Horsehope Craig hoard, only the latter included evidence of more refined sword production.': Claims sword evidence in both hoards. But the 16th-century BCE Llanarmon-yn-Iâl hoard predates swords entirely — there should be no sword evidence there. ✗
  • Choice B — 'hoards discovered before 1865, no such evidence was found in hoards that were discovered later.': Bases the pattern on discovery dates (1865 vs. 1982) rather than artifact dates. Discovery date is irrelevant to whether swords were invented yet. ✗
  • Choice C — 'the Llanarmon-yn-Iâl hoard, no intact swords were found among the items in the hoard.': Claims sword evidence in the earlier hoard, which predates sword development. This reverses the correct relationship. ✗
  • Choice D — 'the Horsehope Craig hoard, no such evidence was found in the Llanarmon-yn-Iâl hoard.': Evidence in the 7th-century BCE hoard (after swords were developed) but not in the 16th-century BCE hoard (before swords were developed). Exactly what the timeline predicts. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
